title: AI Hackers Are Targeting Ordinary People Now, Not Just Big Tech | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
summary: Damien Lewke, founder of Nebulock, argues that AI has drastically lowered the barrier to entry for cyberattacks, enabling small teams with a GPU to execute complex attacks that previously required elite specialists. He advocates for an "assume breach" defensive posture and outlines behavioral signals that indicate attackers are already inside networks.
writeup: In this EO interview, Nebulock founder Damien Lewke argues that AI has lowered the barrier to entry for cyberattacks, letting small teams with a GPU run complex operations that once required elite specialists. The shift matters because it widens the pool of viable targets beyond large tech firms to ordinary people and organizations. Lewke makes the case for an "assume breach" posture, working from the premise that attackers are already inside the network rather than only guarding the perimeter. He outlines behavioral signals that surface intruders, drawing on concepts including the cyber kill chain, threat hunting, and vulnerability detection.
